notes: "SDL_AUDIODRIVER=winmm %command%" as a launch option to get audio in the cutscenes. Avoid proton3.16 beta (for now, at least). Visionaire Config Tool works installing wine-mono, but it is not really necessary. If Steam keeps trying to install .net every time you can delete the contents of ".../steamapps/common/Steamworks Shared/_CommonRedist/DotNet/3.5/" (not extremely elegant, but it can be recovered at anytime clicking on "verify integrity of game files...").
title: Memoria

notes: No sound in cutscenes as others have reported but otherwise fully playable.
title: Memoria

notes: For sound in cutscenes use "SDL_AUDIODRIVER=winmm %command%" as a launch option and proton 3.16-4.
title: Memoria

notes: I first used Proton 4.2.7, game ran fine except for no audio in the cutscenes. Then I used the launch command "SDL_AUDIODRIVER=winmm %command%" as recommended by other reports. This did not fix the audio in the cut scenes. I then tried Proton 3.16.4 and it worked flawlessly, including audio. Game runs perfectly with no issues now.
title: Memoria

notes: No sound in cutscenes. Fix recommend by other users didn't work me (I tried different Proton versions). Otherwise perfectly playable. Full playthrough possible.
title: Memoria
